title: Eriodendron anfractuosum
intro: Eriodendron anfractuosum, commonly known as the "Talaum palmatum" or "Golden Shower Tree", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its ability to treat fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. The bioactive constituents of Eriodendron anfractuosum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Eriodendron anfractuosum is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
taxonomy: Eriodendron anfractuosum is classified within the family Caesalpiniaceae, which is a family of flowering plants. It belongs to the genus Eriodendron and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The species Eriodendron anfractuosum is a type of tree, also known as the Brazilian coral tree. The classification of Eriodendron anfractuosum is further divided into several subgroups, reflecting its unique characteristics and adaptations. As a member of the Caesalpiniaceae family, Eriodendron anfractuosum shares similarities with other related species.

properties: Eriodendron anfractuosum, also known as the West Indian mahogany, has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties. The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Additionally, studies suggest that Eriodendron anfractuosum may possess cardio-protective and neuroprotective properties, which could be beneficial in the prevention and treatment of various diseases. constituents: Eriodendron anfractuosum contains flavonoids, triterpenes, and other bioactive compounds. These active constituents are responsible for its medicinal properties. parts: Eriodendron anfractuosum, also known as the Brazilian coral tree, is a plant species native to Brazil. Its medicinal parts are primarily the seeds, which have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including reducing inflammation and treating skin conditions. The seeds are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that contribute to their medicinal properties. Traditionally, the seeds are crushed and applied topically to the affected area or consumed as a decoction. preparations: Eriodendron anfractuosum is a plant species native to Brazil, and its herbal preparations are mainly used for traditional medicine purposes. The plant's extracts are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues.
